name: On-Premise Enterprise
description: A traditional on-premise enterprise architecture with multiple network security zones separated by firewalls and network segmentation. Common in regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government) where data sovereignty, physical security, and compliance require infrastructure to remain within organizational premises. Relies on perimeter defense, VLAN segmentation, and defense-in-depth network controls.

zones.name: external
zones.description: All external network traffic including internet-facing services, remote employee access via VPN, partner connections, and inbound email. Treated as hostile until authenticated and filtered through the DMZ.

zones.name: dmz
zones.description: The network DMZ between the external firewall and internal firewall. Hosts publicly accessible services that mediate between external and internal networks. Designed to limit blast radius if a DMZ host is compromised.

zones.name: corporate-network
zones.description: The general corporate network hosting end-user workstations, internal applications, and shared services. Users are authenticated via Active Directory. Network Access Control (NAC) enforces device compliance before granting access.

zones.name: datacenter
zones.description: The secure datacenter hosting production application servers, databases, and critical infrastructure. Physically restricted access with environmental controls. Network access limited to specific VLANs and port-level ACLs.

zones.name: management-network
zones.description: The isolated management network used for system administration, monitoring, and security operations. Accessible only through jump boxes with privileged access management. All sessions are recorded and audited.

boundaries.name: Corporate Network to Management Network
controls: Jump box / bastion host access only

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph external["External (Untrusted)"]
        internet[Internet Users]
        remote[Remote Workers]
        partner_vpn[Partner VPN]
    end
    subgraph dmz["DMZ (Semi-Trusted)"]
        fw_ext[External Firewall]
        web(Web Server / Reverse Proxy)
        email(Email Gateway)
        vpn(VPN Concentrator)
    end
    subgraph corp["Corporate Network (Trusted)"]
        fw_int[Internal Firewall]
        ws[Workstations]
        intranet(Internal Web Apps)
        fileshare[(File Shares)]
        ad(Active Directory)
    end
    subgraph dc["Datacenter (Highly Trusted)"]
        appserver(Application Servers)
        dbserver[(Database Servers)]
        storage[(Storage Arrays)]
        hsm(HSM)
        backup[(Backup Systems)]
    end
    subgraph mgmt["Management Network (Highly Trusted)"]
        jumpbox(Jump Box / Bastion)
        siem(SIEM)
        pam(PAM Solution)
        patch(Patch Management)
    end
    internet -->|HTTPS| fw_ext
    remote -->|VPN| fw_ext
    partner_vpn -->|Site-to-Site VPN| fw_ext
    fw_ext --> web
    fw_ext --> email
    fw_ext --> vpn
    web -->|Filtered| fw_int
    vpn -->|Authenticated| fw_int
    fw_int --> ws
    fw_int --> intranet
    ws --> ad
    ws --> fileshare
    intranet -->|ACL| appserver
    appserver -->|SQL/TLS| dbserver
    appserver --> storage
    dbserver --> backup
    appserver --> hsm
    ws -->|Jump Box Only| jumpbox
    jumpbox -->|PAM| pam
    jumpbox --> siem
    jumpbox --> patch
    siem -.->|Monitor| dc
    siem -.->|Monitor| corp
